title: Do not call out to runc for sync
body:
An initial attempt to solve #1574 

Presently, for almost all API operations, we update the container's state using runc. This is very expensive (a full fork/exec for every container involved in an API operation like `ps` adds up very quickly). Also, we're seeing issues when running large quantities of runc processes (as in #1574).

This attempts to resolve this by replacing the expensive call to runc with a much faster `stat()` call to check for the existence of the container exit file created by Conmon when the container exits (and even this can be avoided in most container states).

I'd like to also introduce a --force-sync flag (or something similar) to force a full sync against runc to prevent desync from occurring, but that will come later.

comments.body: @baude This might do exciting things for our perf - particularly things like `ps`
I think code here is (mostly) done, but I don't really know where it stands with passing the tests, so I'll leave the WIP tag on for now
LGTM

Test failures are probably related to an inability to get container PID - previously, every single syncContainer() would get us an accurate PID (full status from runc includes it), but now we need to retrieve it when we start the container, and it seems like for some pod-related things it's not being recorded
All issues seem to be with rootless. Investigating further.

Alright, papr tests seem to be passing. Progress!

:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1715) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@mheon latest Cirrus run says: 'Timed out!' (currently set a 2-hours / normally runs in 30-min).  Cirrus has a pretty-reliable watchdog, so this failure is most likely what it says it is.  Something "hung" during execution of the integration tests.  Jhon's runner script emits the logs after exit, so if the tests hang and timeout, we loose all the details that would be useful to see :confounded: (doh!)

@jwhonce For this specific PR, what do you think about duct-tape (in this PR) for troubleshooting:  Backgrounding a ``tail -f`` of the logs, before executing ``python3 -m unittest...``?
@cevich It seems to be a 50/50 chance that the tests fail, or hang. I'm pretty confident something happening with runc in both cases.

@cevich It would be cleaner to move the showlog's into the cleanup function instead.  Assuming the VM is closed down nicely and not just shot in the head.  Otherwise the output will be interleaved (logs vs test runner output) and very hard to read.

@mheon That integration test does both a start and a restart pod.
FYI --  Neither of which have timeouts.  Which would help in this instance.

> Otherwise the output will be interleaved (logs vs test runner output) and very hard to read.

Ya, trouble here is the thing hangs during the test, so we would never get to ``cleanup()``.  

> Neither of which have timeouts.

Oh, that's a better idea.  Another (maybe simpler) option, could be to run the tests using the `timeout 60m` shell command.  Then we always get to see the logs no matter what and you don't need to add timeouts on a test-by-test basis.

> I'm pretty confident something happening with runc in both cases.

okay @mheon I'll bow-out then, lemmie know if/how I can help.
Needs #1762 merged first, then I'll rebase on top of that - should fix the tests
Alright, rebased on top of #1762, tests should hopefully go green now.
/lgtm
